evals.prompt: Validate the ground truth for DSVW. The ground truth is at realvuln/ground-truth/RealVuln-DSVW/ground-truth.json and the repo is cloned at repos/RealVuln-DSVW/. Check if we're missing any vulnerabilities that are documented in the DSVW README or known from security writeups.
evals.expected_output: A markdown report listing all vulns found in the DSVW README, cross-referenced against the 19 vulns + 4 FP traps in the ground truth. Should identify any gaps and confirm which GT entries are backed by external sources.
expectations: A markdown report file was saved to the outputs directory
The report lists all 19 true vulnerabilities from the ground truth
The report mentions the 4 false-positive traps
The report includes a coverage-by-vulnerability-class table or breakdown
The report references the DSVW GitHub README as a source
The report includes a sources/references section with URLs

evals.prompt: I just finished writing the ground truth for DSVW (realvuln/ground-truth/RealVuln-DSVW/ground-truth.json). Can you check if it's complete? The actual code is in repos/RealVuln-DSVW/. I want to make sure I haven't missed anything obvious.
evals.expected_output: Should read the GT, review the dsvw.py source code for dangerous patterns, search the web for DSVW vulnerability documentation, and produce a gap analysis. Should note the FP trap ratio (4 traps for 19 vulns = good ratio).
expectations: A markdown report file was saved to the outputs directory
The report shows evidence of having read and analyzed dsvw.py source code
The report mentions the false-positive trap ratio (4 traps for 19 vulns)
The report cross-references dangerous code patterns against GT entries
The report searches the web or README for external validation

evals.prompt: Run a GT validation on the DSVW ground truth. I specifically want to know: are there any CVEs for DSVW that we haven't captured? And does the README list any vuln types that our ground truth is missing?
evals.expected_output: Should search NVD/CVE databases for DSVW, fetch the GitHub README, and compare against the GT. DSVW is a tiny educational app so there probably aren't formal CVEs, but the README should list all the intentional vulns. Report should clearly state whether CVEs exist or not.
expectations: A markdown report file was saved to the outputs directory
The report explicitly addresses whether any CVEs exist for DSVW
The report fetches and analyzes the DSVW README content
The report maps each README-listed vulnerability to a corresponding GT entry
The report directly answers both user questions (CVEs and README gaps)
